generated_requirements.text: The system shall operate in a fully functional offline mode, caching the most recently synchronized MLS listings, client contacts, calendar entries, and CMA data locally on the device, and shall automatically sync all changes made offline once network connectivity is restored.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) such that cardmember services representatives can only initiate, view, and modify dispute cases associated with cardholders within their explicitly authorized cardholder base; any attempt to access a case outside this boundary shall be denied and logged.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly states that the system enforces role-based access controls tied to an authorized cardholder base, making this a core security requirement with direct regulatory implications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control such that merchant services representatives can only initiate, view, and modify dispute cases associated with merchants within their authorized merchant portfolio; access to cases outside this portfolio shall be denied and logged.
generated_requirements.rationale: Merchant services representatives operate on a distinct authorized boundary (merchant portfolio) that must be enforced separately from the cardmember boundary.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide system administrators with the ability to create, modify, deactivate, and assign roles and permissions for all user accounts, including defining and updating each user's authorized cardholder or merchant base.
generated_requirements.rationale: System administrators require explicit user and role management capabilities to maintain the RBAC model described in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to search for existing dispute cases using at least the following criteria: cardholder account number, merchant ID, transaction date range, dispute case ID, case status, and dispute type (ticket retrieval or chargeback).
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case identifies searching for existing disputes as a primary function; multi-criteria search is essential for efficient case management.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized cardmember services representatives and merchant services representatives to create a ticket retrieval request against a disputed transaction, capturing at minimum: transaction identifier, disputed amount, dispute reason code, and initiating user identity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ticket retrieval request creation is an explicitly named core function of the Disputes application.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to create a chargeback notification against a disputed transaction, capturing at minimum: transaction identifier, chargeback reason code, disputed amount, supporting documentation references, and initiating user identity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Chargeback notification creation is an explicitly named core function of the Disputes application.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall manage each dispute case through a defined lifecycle with at least the following discrete states: Initiated, Pending Documentation, Under Review, Chargeback Filed, Resolved, and Closed; and shall enforce valid state transitions according to configured business rules, preventing any transition not defined in the workflow.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case describes end-to-end lifecycle management; explicit state modeling ensures process integrity and supports regulatory compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically calculate and display regulatory deadline dates for each open dispute case based on the applicable regulation (Regulation E for consumer electronic fund transfers, Regulation Z for credit card billing disputes, and Merchant Operating Regulations for merchant-side cases), and shall generate an alert to the assigned representative and their supervisor when a case is within 5 business days of a regulatory deadline.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulation E, Regulation Z, and Merchant Operating Regulations impose hard timing constraints; automated deadline tracking and alerting is required to ensure compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent any state transition or case closure that would result in a regulatory deadline being missed, displaying a blocking warning to the user and requiring supervisor override with a recorded justification before proceeding.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulatory mandates under Reg E, Reg Z, and Merchant Operating Regulations impose hard functional constraints that must be enforced at the workflow level.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a complete, immutable audit trail for every dispute case, recording all user-initiated and system-generated events including: event type, timestamp (UTC), actor identity (user ID or system process name), previous state, new state, and a summary of data changed; the audit trail shall be retained for a minimum of 7 years.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly requires a complete audit trail of all activity; immutability and long-term retention are necessary for regulatory compliance and dispute resolution evidence.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the cardmember database and merchant database to retrieve account holder information, transaction history, and account status in real time when a dispute case is created or searched; if either database is unavailable, the system shall display a clear error message, prevent case creation, and log the integration failure without corrupting existing case data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integration with cardmember and merchant databases is required for case initiation; failure-handling behavior must be defined to protect data integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the billing system to submit financial adjustment requests (credits, debits, and reversals) when a chargeback or resolution action is processed; the system shall implement idempotent submission to prevent duplicate financial adjustments, and shall reconcile and alert on any unacknowledged adjustment requests within 15 minutes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Financial adjustments via the billing system are a core integration requirement; idempotency and reconciliation protect financial data integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the letter printing utility to generate and dispatch merchant correspondence (e.g., chargeback notices, retrieval request letters) at defined workflow trigger points; if the letter printing utility is unavailable, the system shall queue the correspondence request, retry at configurable intervals, and alert an administrator if the request remains undelivered after 1 hour.
generated_requirements.rationale: Letter generation is an explicitly named integration; queuing and retry logic ensures correspondence is not lost during outages, which is critical for regulatory notification deadlines.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return dispute case search results within 3 seconds for queries returning up to 500 records under normal load conditions (up to 200 concurrent users), and shall return results within 8 seconds under peak load conditions (up to 500 concurrent users).
generated_requirements.rationale: Case management efficiency for internal and external representatives depends on responsive search; explicit thresholds under normal and peak load ensure usability at scale.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be available 99.9% of the time during defined business hours (06:00–22:00 local time, Monday–Saturday), with planned maintenance windows restricted to off-peak hours and communicated at least 48 hours in advance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dispute processing is time-sensitive due to regulatory deadlines; high availability during business hours is essential for both internal and external representatives.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the user's browser and the application server, and between the application server and all back-end integration systems, shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher; storage of sensitive cardholder and merchant data at rest shall use AES-256 encryption.
generated_requirements.rationale: Credit card dispute data is highly sensitive PII and financial data; encryption in transit and at rest is a baseline security and regulatory compliance requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall lock a user account after 5 consecutive failed authentication attempts and require administrator intervention or a verified self-service reset process to unlock it; all authentication events (successful and failed) shall be recorded in the audit log.
generated_requirements.rationale: Account lockout protects against brute-force attacks on a system containing sensitive financial and cardholder data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present all case management workflows, forms, and status indicators through a web-based interface that conforms to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards, and shall provide inline validation messages and contextual help text on all data entry forms to minimize user error.
generated_requirements.rationale: The application is used by both internal and external representatives across varying technical proficiency levels; accessibility compliance and guided data entry reduce errors and training burden.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on any major cloud or on-premises infrastructure supporting containerized workloads (Docker/Kubernetes), and shall not embed any hard-coded environment-specific configuration values, with all environment parameters supplied via externalized configuration.
generated_requirements.rationale: Containerized, environment-agnostic deployment supports operational flexibility and future infrastructure migrations without application rework.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase shall maintain a minimum unit and integration test coverage of 80%, all integration points with back-end systems shall be abstracted behind versioned interface contracts, and all configuration of business rules (including regulatory deadline calculations) shall be externalized to allow updates without code deployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulatory rules and business processes change frequently; high test coverage and externalized configuration reduce the risk and cost of ongoing compliance-driven changes.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide Search functionality. In addition to the above criteria the search functionality must further allow the user to limit the results of the search by a date range the type of dispute (ticket retrieval request or chargeback notification) the case status (open closed or all) and the dispute reason code.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: A list of all disputes cases that meet the entered search criteria must be provided to the user. The list of cases must differentiate the type and/or status of the case by color -coding the line item in the list. For example all open cases will be yellow and all closed cases will be gray.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall provide the users the ability to view the history of all activities that have occurred on the dispute case.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Dispute System shall provide the users with the ability to follow up on a dispute case via follow-up actions. The list of available follow up actions for a given dispute case must be filtered by the status of the case and the access level of the user.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall record the name of the user and the date for any activity that creates or modifies the disputes case in the system. A detailed history of the actions taken on the case including the date and the user that performed the action must be maintained for auditing purposes.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: For any systematic (non-user initiated) action that occurs on a case such as the disputes aging process a case activity will be generated. Upon the next logon the user that initiated the dispute case must be notified that a systematic action has occurred on the dispute case.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: All letter requests must be formatted according to guidelines specified by the Print Letter Utility system.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Any disputes cases that have been closed for over 6 months must be purged from the online disputes database.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a role-based access control (RBAC) mechanism that restricts each user to only the functions and data permitted for their assigned role (collision estimator, adjuster, or supervisory adjuster).
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly requires that each user type interacts only with functions appropriate to their responsibilities, necessitating enforced role boundaries.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow collision estimators to search the recycled parts inventory sourced from the ChoiceParts external data feed by part type, part number, vehicle make, model, and year, and display availability and supplier information for each matching result.
generated_requirements.rationale: Collision estimators must be able to locate recycled parts from preferred suppliers as a core workflow step before applying them to estimates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow collision estimators to add one or more recycled parts retrieved from the ChoiceParts inventory to a vehicle repair estimate, recording the part identifier, supplier, unit cost, and quantity for each added part.
generated_requirements.rationale: Applying recycled parts to collision estimates is the primary functional responsibility of the collision estimator role.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow adjusters to assign a preferred repair facility to an insurance claim prior to estimate review.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adjusters are responsible for directing claims to appropriate repair facilities, which is a defined handoff point in the workflow.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow adjusters to review completed vehicle repair estimates, including all line items, recycled parts applied, associated costs, and the originating collision estimator, and to approve or reject each estimate with a mandatory written justification.
generated_requirements.rationale: Estimate review and approval/rejection is a core adjuster responsibility and a critical handoff point between the estimator and adjuster roles.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow adjusters to request an audit report for a specified claim or repair facility, which evaluates the proportion of recycled parts used versus new parts, flags deviations from recycled-parts usage targets, and identifies opportunities where recycled parts could have been substituted.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit report generation is an explicit adjuster function described in the use case, directly supporting the business policy of maximising recycled parts usage.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow supervisory adjusters to assign a numeric rating (on a defined 1–5 scale) to a repair facility following the completion of an audit, capturing the rating, the auditing adjuster, the date, and any supporting comments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supervisory adjusters rating repair facilities based on audit outcomes is an explicitly described function that informs future facility assignments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronise recycled parts inventory data from the ChoiceParts external data feed at intervals not exceeding 15 minutes, ensuring that search results presented to collision estimators reflect near-real-time availability.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data freshness is a key quality attribute; stale inventory data could cause estimators to apply parts that are no longer available, creating downstream rework.

generated_requirements.text: When the ChoiceParts data feed is unavailable or returns an error, the system shall display a clearly visible warning to the collision estimator indicating the last successful synchronisation timestamp, continue to serve the most recently cached inventory data, and log the failure event with full error details for administrator review.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integration failure handling is a critical reliability concern; estimators must be informed of data staleness and operations must degrade gracefully rather than halt.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce strict multi-tenant data isolation such that claims, estimates, repair facility assignments, audit reports, and ratings belonging to one insurance company are never accessible to users authenticated under a different insurance company's tenant, enforced at both the application and database layers.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly requires that claims data for multiple insurance companies be kept strictly isolated; a breach of isolation would constitute a serious confidentiality violation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a tamper-evident audit trail that records every create, update, delete, and status-change event on claims, estimates, parts selections, facility assignments, and ratings, capturing the acting user, their role, tenant identifier, timestamp, and before/after field values.
generated_requirements.rationale: Auditability and traceability are key quality attributes; insurance claims processing is a regulated domain requiring full accountability for all data changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return recycled parts search results to the collision estimator within 3 seconds under normal operating conditions for queries returning up to 200 results.
generated_requirements.rationale: Estimators work in both office and repair shop environments where responsiveness directly impacts productivity; a 3-second threshold aligns with standard interactive usability benchmarks.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall be operable on standard desktop browsers and on tablet devices with screen widths of 768 px or greater, without requiring the installation of additional plugins or native applications.
generated_requirements.rationale: The system is designed for use in both office and repair shop environments, requiring a UI that functions across device form factors without complex deployment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an availability of at least 99.5% measured on a rolling 30-day basis, excluding pre-announced maintenance windows not exceeding 4 hours per month.
generated_requirements.rationale: Claims processing is a time-sensitive business operation; extended downtime directly impacts adjuster and estimator productivity and may breach SLAs with insurance company clients.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present collision estimators with a ranked list of recycled parts results that prioritises parts from the insurance company's designated preferred suppliers before displaying results from non-preferred suppliers.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case specifies that estimators search for parts from preferred suppliers, implying a business rule that preferred supplier inventory must be surfaced preferentially.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent a collision estimator from submitting an estimate for adjuster review unless at least one recycled part has been evaluated (either applied or explicitly marked as 'not available') for each labour operation line item where a recycled part match exists in the ChoiceParts inventory.
generated_requirements.rationale: This business rule enforces the recycled parts usage policy by ensuring estimators actively consider recycled alternatives before finalising estimates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow supervisory adjusters to view aggregated repair facility performance dashboards showing average recycled-parts usage rates, average audit ratings, and claim volume per facility, filterable by date range and insurance company tenant.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supervisory adjusters need consolidated performance data to make informed facility assignment recommendations, extending the rating function into actionable oversight.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ensure that all data in transit between clients and the server, and between the server and the ChoiceParts data feed, is encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Insurance claims data is sensitive personal and financial information; encryption in transit is a baseline security control required to protect data confidentiality.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed with a modular architecture that allows the ChoiceParts integration adapter to be replaced or updated independently of core claims processing logic, with integration configuration (endpoints, credentials, polling intervals) managed via environment-level configuration files rather than hard-coded values.
generated_requirements.rationale: External data feed contracts and endpoints change over time; isolating the integration layer reduces the cost and risk of future changes to the ChoiceParts feed or its replacement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide contextual inline guidance and field-level tooltips throughout the estimate creation and review workflows, ensuring that users with no prior system training can complete core tasks without consulting external documentation.
generated_requirements.rationale: The system is used across office and repair shop environments by users with varying technical proficiency; embedded guidance reduces training overhead and error rates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow employees to search for available conference rooms by specifying date, time range, building, floor, capacity, and required equipment, and shall display a list of matching available rooms in real time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Core employee workflow: finding a suitable room is the primary entry point for the reservation process and must support rich filtering to be useful across multi-building organizations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow meeting organizers to create a meeting by defining a title, agenda, date, time, duration, recurrence pattern, required room, and a list of invited attendees, and shall send email invitations to all invitees upon confirmation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Captures the full meeting-creation workflow for the organizer role, including agenda management and the integration touchpoint with the email server.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow invited attendees to accept, tentatively accept, or decline meeting invitations from within the email notification or from within the web application, and shall update the organizer's attendee status view in real time upon each response.
generated_requirements.rationale: Closes the invitation loop for the organizer and mirrors standard calendar-platform behavior expected by enterprise users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically enforce room reservation conflict rules, preventing double-booking of any conference room and immediately notifying the requestor if their requested time slot is unavailable, suggesting the next three available slots for the same room.
generated_requirements.rationale: Conflict resolution is a critical business rule; automatic enforcement removes administrative overhead and the alternative-slot suggestion improves usability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow employees and organizers to reserve specific equipment items (e.g., projectors, video-conferencing units, whiteboards) for a meeting, track the equipment's availability and current location, and generate transport requests when equipment must be moved between rooms or floors.
generated_requirements.rationale: Equipment tracking and transport requests are explicitly called out in the use case as distinct workflows that must be supported alongside room reservations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send configurable reminder notifications to meeting organizers and accepted attendees via email at user-defined intervals before a meeting (e.g., 24 hours, 1 hour), and shall support in-application notification banners as a secondary channel.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reminders reduce no-shows and room waste; configurability of intervals accommodates varying organizational policies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display interactive building maps for each registered office location, visually indicating each conference room's real-time availability status (available, occupied, reserved) and allowing users to click a room on the map to view its details or initiate a reservation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Visual map navigation is explicitly described in the use case and significantly improves discoverability of rooms in large, multi-floor buildings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide managers with a reporting module that generates reservation utilization reports filterable by building, floor, room, date range, and organizer, and exports results in at least CSV and PDF formats.
generated_requirements.rationale: Managers require elevated access to operational data for capacity planning and policy enforcement; export formats support downstream analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support role-based access control with at minimum three distinct roles — Employee, Meeting Organizer, and Manager — each with a defined, non-overlapping set of permissions, and shall prevent users from performing actions outside their assigned role.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly distinguishes three actor types with different capabilities; RBAC is the standard mechanism to enforce these boundaries securely.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with at least one external SMTP-compatible email server for sending and receiving meeting invitations, confirmations, reminders, and cancellation notices, with configurable server host, port, authentication credentials, and TLS settings.
generated_requirements.rationale: Email integration is a primary external touchpoint; configurability of connection parameters is required to support diverse enterprise email infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support full Unicode character encoding (UTF-8) and shall provide a localization framework that allows the user interface, date/time formats, number formats, and system-generated notifications to be rendered in any configured locale, with English provided as the default and at least French, German, and Spanish provided at initial release.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly targets global, multilingual organizations; locale support is a key quality attribute and a prerequisite for international deployment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall sustain a throughput of at least 500 concurrent active user sessions without degradation of response time, and all room search and reservation confirmation operations shall complete within 2 seconds at the 95th percentile under this load.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enterprise-grade positioning and large concurrent user base require explicit, measurable performance targets to validate scalability under realistic load.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce transport-layer security (TLS 1.2 or higher) for all browser-to-server communications, shall store all user passwords using an adaptive hashing algorithm (e.g., bcrypt), and shall implement session timeout after a configurable period of inactivity (default: 30 minutes).
generated_requirements.rationale: Enterprise applications handling organizational scheduling data are high-value targets; encryption in transit and secure credential storage are baseline security controls.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an availability of 99.5% or greater during defined business hours (configurable per time zone), with planned maintenance windows scheduled outside those hours and communicated to administrators at least 48 hours in advance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Meeting scheduling is a time-sensitive, business-critical function; downtime during business hours directly disrupts organizational operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on any standard Java EE (Jakarta EE 10+) compliant application server and shall support at least two major ANSI SQL-compliant relational database management systems (e.g., PostgreSQL, Oracle, Microsoft SQL Server) via a configurable JDBC data source, with no hard-coded database vendor dependencies in application code.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enterprise customers operate heterogeneous infrastructure; database and application server portability is essential for broad adoption and reduces vendor lock-in.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an administration console through which authorized administrators can configure organizational branding (logo, color scheme, application name) without modifying application code or redeploying the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly requires brandability; runtime configurability of branding eliminates the need for custom builds per customer, reducing deployment and maintenance cost.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase shall adhere to documented coding standards, include unit test coverage of at least 80% for all business-logic modules, and provide a configuration-driven architecture such that adding a new building, room type, or equipment category requires only data entry in the administration console and no code changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: A multi-year lifecycle with minimal administrative overhead demands high test coverage to catch regressions and a data-driven configuration model to accommodate organizational growth.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be fully operable through any modern, standards-compliant web browser (current and previous major versions of Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Safari) without requiring the installation of browser plugins, extensions, or non-standard runtimes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Browser accessibility is a stated requirement; plugin-free operation ensures compatibility across managed enterprise desktops where plugin installation may be restricted.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support multi-tenancy at the organizational-unit level, allowing a single deployment to serve multiple distinct business units or subsidiaries, each with isolated data visibility, independent branding, and separately configurable room and equipment inventories.
generated_requirements.rationale: Global organizations often have distinct subsidiaries or business units; multi-tenancy on a single deployment reduces infrastructure cost while maintaining data segregation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose a documented RESTful API (OpenAPI 3.0 specification) for all core reservation, room, and user-management operations, enabling integration with third-party calendar platforms (e.g., Microsoft Exchange, Google Calendar) and future client applications.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enterprise environments require interoperability with existing calendar ecosystems; a documented REST API is the standard integration mechanism and future-proofs the system against evolving client needs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically generate an Inventory Quantity Adjustment (IQA) document at the end of each business day by calculating ingredient consumption based on recorded POS sales quantities and the corresponding product formulas.
generated_requirements.rationale: Core business rule: daily sales data must drive automatic inventory deduction to eliminate manual calculation errors and ensure stock accuracy.

generated_requirements.text: When generating an IQA document, the system shall evaluate substitutionary ingredients in a defined priority order and select the highest-priority substitute whose current inventory level is sufficient to cover the required quantity before falling back to the next substitute.
generated_requirements.rationale: Substitution logic must be deterministic and stock-aware to prevent over-deduction of unavailable ingredients and reflect real kitchen operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow formula administrators to define product formulas that include one or more ingredients, each with a specified quantity and unit of measure, and optionally one or more ranked substitutionary ingredients per formula line.
generated_requirements.rationale: Flexible formula definition with substitution support is the foundational data structure that drives all downstream inventory calculations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the definition of semi-products (intermediate preparations) within product formulas, allowing a semi-product to be referenced as an ingredient in one or more finished-product formulas and itself composed of raw ingredients with their own quantities.
generated_requirements.rationale: Semi-products represent real kitchen workflows (e.g., sauces, doughs) and must be modelled to ensure accurate multi-level ingredient consumption calculations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow menu administrators to create and maintain hierarchical product menus with at least two levels of categorisation (e.g., category → sub-category → product), and associate each leaf-level product with exactly one active product formula.
generated_requirements.rationale: Hierarchical menus reflect how products are organised on POS terminals; the formula association is required for IQA generation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronise the active product menu to all registered POS terminals, ensuring that any menu change published by an administrator is reflected on POS terminals within 60 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: POS operators must always work from the current menu to ensure sales data maps correctly to formulas during IQA generation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) with at least three distinct roles — Inventory Manager, Formula Administrator, and POS Operator — where each role is granted access only to the functional modules and data operations explicitly assigned to it.
generated_requirements.rationale: Segregation of duties is required to prevent unauthorised modifications to formulas, inventory records, or sales data across staff personas.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record a tamper-evident audit log entry for every create, update, delete, or approval action performed on product formulas, menus, inventory adjustments, and user accounts, capturing the user identity, timestamp, action type, and before/after field values.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit-compliant accounting standards require a complete, attributable change history for all financially relevant records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow an Inventory Manager to manually review, annotate, and approve or reject a generated IQA document before it is posted to the inventory ledger, and shall prevent any unapproved IQA document from affecting inventory balances.
generated_requirements.rationale: A human approval gate ensures data integrity and compliance; inventory balances must only reflect verified adjustments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete the end-of-day IQA document generation process, including substitution resolution for all sold products, within 5 minutes of initiation for a daily sales volume of up to 2,000 line items.
generated_requirements.rationale: End-of-day processing must finish within a practical window to allow managers to review and approve adjustments before the next business day begins.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall respond to any interactive user action (e.g., saving a formula, loading a menu, querying inventory levels) within 3 seconds under normal operating conditions with up to 20 concurrent users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Acceptable response times are critical for staff productivity, particularly for POS operators serving customers in real time.

generated_requirements.text: All data in transit between client workstations, POS terminals, and the server shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher, and all passwords shall be stored using an adaptive hashing algorithm (e.g., bcrypt or Argon2) with a minimum work factor appropriate to current hardware.
generated_requirements.rationale: Protecting credentials and business-sensitive data (formulas, sales figures, inventory levels) from interception and unauthorised access is a baseline security requirement.

generated_requirements.text: User sessions shall automatically expire after a configurable period of inactivity (default: 15 minutes), requiring re-authentication before further actions are permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic session expiry reduces the risk of unauthorised access on shared or unattended workstations common in restaurant environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable and fully operational on existing Windows 10 (or later) hardware without requiring hardware upgrades, targeting a minimum specification of a dual-core CPU at 2 GHz, 4 GB RAM, and 50 GB available disk space for the server node.
generated_requirements.rationale: The business constraint of running on existing hardware must be formalised to prevent solution designs that require capital expenditure on new equipment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an availability of at least 99.5% during defined business hours (06:00–24:00 local time), with planned maintenance windows scheduled outside these hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: Restaurant operations span long daily windows; system downtime during service directly impacts sales recording and inventory accuracy.

generated_requirements.text: In the event of a network failure between a POS terminal and the central server, the POS terminal shall continue to record sales transactions locally and automatically synchronise all offline transactions to the server upon reconnection, without data loss.
generated_requirements.rationale: Network interruptions must not halt sales operations or cause gaps in the sales data that feeds IQA generation.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall follow a consistent visual design language across all modules, provide inline validation messages for all data-entry forms, and require no more than 3 navigation steps to reach any primary function from the main dashboard.
generated_requirements.rationale: Staff with varying technical proficiency must be able to operate the system efficiently with minimal training, reducing input errors and onboarding time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform automated daily backups of the entire database, retain backups for a minimum of 30 days, and support point-in-time recovery to within 1 hour of any failure event.
generated_requirements.rationale: Formula, inventory, and audit data are business-critical; robust backup and recovery capabilities protect against data loss from hardware failure or corruption.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase shall be structured using clearly separated modules (e.g., formula management, menu management, inventory, POS integration, reporting), with each module independently testable and documented, to facilitate future enhancements and bug fixes.
generated_requirements.rationale: A modular architecture reduces the cost and risk of future changes, particularly as business rules around substitution logic and menu structures evolve.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 20 concurrent authenticated users — spanning all roles — without degradation of response times beyond the thresholds defined in R011, and shall be architected to scale to 50 concurrent users through configuration changes alone (e.g., connection pool tuning) without code modifications.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-branch or growing restaurant operations may onboard additional staff; the system must accommodate growth without requiring re-engineering.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow new customers to register an account by providing a username, email address, password, and country of residence, and shall send a verification email before activating the account.
generated_requirements.rationale: Account registration is the entry point of the customer journey; email verification ensures account authenticity and reduces fraudulent sign-ups.

generated_requirements.text: During registration, the system shall present the customer with a choice between two subscription plans: (1) Pay-As-You-Go, where the customer is charged per individual movie purchase, and (2) Unlimited, where the customer pays a recurring monthly fee for unrestricted access to the full catalog.
generated_requirements.rationale: The dual subscription model is a core business differentiator; capturing plan selection at registration drives all downstream billing and access-control logic.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall grant a Pay-As-You-Go customer access to a purchased movie for exactly 48 hours from the time of purchase, after which the movie shall become inaccessible without a new purchase.
generated_requirements.rationale: The two-day viewing window is a defined business rule that must be enforced consistently to protect content licensing agreements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to search the movie catalog by title, genre, language, release year, and country of origin, and shall display results ranked by relevance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Robust search and discovery is essential for customer satisfaction on a large catalog platform, enabling users to find culturally relevant African content efficiently.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall accept payment via credit/debit card (processed through a PCI-DSS-compliant payment gateway) and via pre-paid cards, where the customer enters a pre-paid card code that is validated against the system's activated card inventory before credit is applied to the account.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supporting both payment methods broadens accessibility, particularly for African users who may lack traditional banking access; pre-paid card validation prevents fraud.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to activate batches of pre-paid cards by uploading a CSV file containing card codes and denominations, and shall mark each card as 'active', 'redeemed', or 'expired' throughout its lifecycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: Administrator-managed pre-paid card activation is required to control card inventory and prevent unauthorized or duplicate redemptions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to add, edit, and remove movies from the catalog, including metadata such as title, synopsis, cast, director, genre, language, country of origin, release year, poster image, and rental/purchase price.
generated_requirements.rationale: Full catalog management by administrators ensures content accuracy and enables timely addition of new African films to the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to submit a star rating (1–5) and a text review for any movie they have purchased, and shall allow administrators to approve, edit, or remove reviews that violate community guidelines.
generated_requirements.rationale: Community reviews drive engagement and trust; moderation capability protects the platform from abusive or inappropriate content.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the Izogn Manager with a real-time analytics dashboard displaying key metrics including total revenue, number of active subscriptions by plan type, top-selling movies, daily/weekly/monthly active users, and pre-paid card redemption rates.
generated_requirements.rationale: Manager-level analytics are required for business decision-making, revenue monitoring, and content strategy planning.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deliver movie streams with a minimum sustained bitrate of 2 Mbps for standard definition (480p) and 5 Mbps for high definition (720p), and shall adapt stream quality dynamically based on the customer's available bandwidth using adaptive bitrate (ABR) streaming.
generated_requirements.rationale: Smooth, adaptive streaming is critical for user retention; ABR ensures a good experience across the varied network conditions common in African markets.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return search results within 2 seconds for 95% of queries under a concurrent load of up to 10,000 active users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fast search response is a key usability expectation; the load threshold reflects anticipated peak usage for a growing streaming platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce HTTPS (TLS 1.2 or higher) for all client-server communications, store customer passwords using a salted cryptographic hash (bcrypt or Argon2), and never transmit or log plaintext passwords or full payment card numbers.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption in transit and secure credential storage are baseline security controls required to protect customer data and maintain platform trust.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement role-based access control (RBAC) with at least three distinct roles — Customer, Administrator, and Manager — ensuring that each role can only access the features and data explicitly assigned to it.
generated_requirements.rationale: RBAC prevents privilege escalation and unauthorized access to sensitive administrative and financial functions.

generated_requirements.text: The platform's user interface shall achieve a minimum score of 80 on the Google Lighthouse Accessibility audit, conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA guidelines, and support right-to-left (RTL) text rendering to accommodate future language expansions across the African continent.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accessibility compliance ensures the platform is usable by people with disabilities and positions it for broader African language support, aligning with the global accessibility goal.

generated_requirements.text: The platform's visual design shall incorporate culturally resonant imagery, color palettes, typography, and iconography reflective of African — and specifically Nigerian — cultural identity, while remaining welcoming and navigable to a global audience unfamiliar with the region.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cultural resonance is a stated strategic goal; a culturally aligned UI increases engagement and brand loyalty among the primary target audience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a monthly uptime of at least 99.5%, measured excluding scheduled maintenance windows, and shall notify customers of planned downtime at least 24 hours in advance via email and an in-app banner.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability is essential for a streaming service where downtime directly translates to lost revenue and customer churn; advance notification manages user expectations.

generated_requirements.text: The system architecture shall support horizontal scaling of both the web application tier and the streaming delivery tier to handle a tenfold increase in concurrent users beyond the initial baseline without degradation in response time or stream quality.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scalability is a key quality attribute; the platform must accommodate rapid user growth typical of viral content events or marketing campaigns in African markets.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with applicable data protection regulations — including Nigeria's NDPR (Nigeria Data Protection Regulation) and the EU GDPR for European users — by obtaining explicit consent before collecting personal data, providing customers with the ability to download or delete their personal data on request, and retaining personal data for no longer than 24 months after account inactivity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulatory compliance with NDPR and GDPR is a legal obligation; data minimization and right-to-erasure features protect customer privacy and avoid regulatory penalties.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement automated daily backups of all customer account data, transaction records, and movie catalog metadata, with backups stored in a geographically separate data center, and shall support full restoration within a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 4 hours and a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of 24 hours.

generated_requirements.text: The system codebase shall follow a modular, service-oriented architecture with documented APIs, maintain at least 80% unit test coverage for core business logic modules (authentication, billing, access control, and streaming entitlement), and include an up-to-date developer wiki to facilitate onboarding and future feature development.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall accept incoming lead submissions from external vendors via a RESTful web service API, supporting JSON payloads that include at minimum: vendor ID, contact information (first name, last name, phone, email), academic interest, and submission timestamp.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defines the primary ingestion interface and minimum required data contract for all incoming leads, establishing the foundation for all downstream processing.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate each incoming lead against a configurable set of corporate-level and school-level parameters, including but not limited to: valid contact information format, geographic eligibility, program availability, and vendor authorization status, before any scoring is performed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ensures only structurally and contextually valid leads proceed to scoring, preventing corrupt or unauthorized data from entering downstream workflows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall compute a composite lead score for each validated lead using a weighted combination of configurable contact criteria (e.g., phone reachability, email validity) and academic criteria (e.g., program match, enrollment readiness), producing a single numeric score on a defined scale (e.g., 0–100).
generated_requirements.rationale: Captures the core scoring logic that drives all routing decisions, requiring a deterministic and auditable calculation method.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall route leads based on their composite score according to configurable thresholds: leads at or above the high-score threshold shall be automatically forwarded to the enterprise system and assigned to an admissions representative; leads between the mid-range lower and upper thresholds shall be placed in a manual review queue for Enrollment Coordinators; leads below the low-score threshold or failing validation shall be rejected and returned to the originating vendor with a machine-readable rejection reason code and human-readable description.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defines the three-tier disposition logic that is the central business function of the system, ensuring every lead receives a deterministic outcome.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect duplicate leads by comparing incoming submissions against previously processed leads using a configurable matching key (e.g., combination of email address, phone number, and school/program), flag duplicates with a DUPLICATE status, store them for reference, and suppress forwarding to the enterprise system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Prevents redundant lead records from polluting the enterprise system and ensures idempotent processing, which is critical for billing accuracy and admissions workflow integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose an interface for vendors to submit credit validation records for rejected leads to the enterprise billing system, including the original lead reference ID, rejection reason code, and vendor-supplied supporting data, and shall return a confirmation or error response for each submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supports the vendor credit workflow downstream of rejection, ensuring financial reconciliation is possible without manual intervention.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a secured administrative interface through which authorized Administrative users can create, read, update, and deactivate scoring parameters at both the corporate and school levels, set and modify score threshold values for high, mid-range, and low dispositions, and manage vendor authorization records.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enables the configurability of all scoring and routing logic without requiring code changes, and restricts sensitive configuration to authorized personnel only.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide Enrollment Coordinators with a manual review queue interface that displays mid-range leads with their composite scores, scoring breakdowns, and contact details, and allows coordinators to approve (forward to enterprise system), reject (return to vendor), or escalate each lead, recording the coordinator's user ID, action taken, and timestamp for every decision.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defines the human-in-the-loop workflow for mid-range leads and mandates the audit trail necessary for compliance and quality review.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and make available defect reports accessible to Administrative users, summarizing lead volumes by disposition (accepted, queued, rejected, duplicate), rejection reason distributions, vendor-level acceptance rates, and scoring parameter effectiveness over configurable date ranges.
generated_requirements.rationale: Provides operational visibility and supports continuous improvement of scoring parameters and vendor relationship management.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete the full processing cycle for a single lead — from receipt through validation, scoring, and disposition routing — within 5 seconds under normal operating load, and within 10 seconds at peak load, to ensure compatibility with downstream auto-representative assignment SLA requirements.
generated_requirements.rationale: Quantifies the strict processing time threshold explicitly called out in the use case description, ensuring the auto-representative assignment workflow is not blocked by lead processing latency.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a sustained throughput of at least 500 concurrent lead submissions per minute without degradation of processing time SLAs, with horizontal scaling capability to handle burst traffic up to 3x the baseline rate.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ensures the system can handle high-volume vendor submission events (e.g., marketing campaign bursts) without violating processing time commitments.

generated_requirements.text: All API endpoints exposed by the system shall require authentication via OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens, and all data in transit shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher. Vendor API credentials shall be stored using a secrets management service and shall never appear in application logs.
generated_requirements.rationale: Protects sensitive prospective student PII and vendor credentials from interception or unauthorized access, addressing the security quality attribute.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) with at minimum three distinct roles — Vendor (API submission only), Enrollment Coordinator (queue review only), and Administrator (full configuration and reporting access) — ensuring no role can access capabilities outside its defined permission set.
generated_requirements.rationale: Prevents privilege escalation and unauthorized data access by strictly partitioning system capabilities by user role, as implied by the multi-role model in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a tamper-evident audit log of all lead lifecycle events, including receipt, validation outcome, score assigned, disposition applied, any manual review actions, and all administrative configuration changes, retaining logs for a minimum of 7 years and making them queryable by authorized administrators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supports auditability requirements for regulatory compliance and internal quality assurance, providing a complete and immutable record of all system actions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.9% (measured monthly), with planned maintenance windows scheduled outside of peak vendor submission hours and communicated to vendors at least 48 hours in advance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ensures the lead ingestion pipeline is reliably accessible to vendors and does not create gaps in lead capture that could impact enrollment targets.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement idempotent lead submission handling such that resubmission of a lead with the same vendor-assigned lead reference ID within a configurable deduplication window (default: 30 days) returns the original processing result without reprocessing or creating a new lead record.
generated_requirements.rationale: Prevents double-processing due to vendor retry logic or network failures, ensuring data integrity in both the lead store and the enterprise billing system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the enterprise system and enterprise billing system via versioned, documented APIs, and shall handle integration failures (e.g., timeouts, 5xx errors) with automatic retry logic using exponential backoff (up to 3 retries), followed by dead-letter queuing and alerting to administrators if all retries are exhausted.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ensures that transient downstream system failures do not result in lost leads or missed billing records, addressing the interoperability and reliability quality attributes.

generated_requirements.text: All scoring parameters, routing thresholds, deduplication window settings, and vendor authorization records shall be configurable by Administrative users through the administrative interface without requiring a system deployment or code change, and configuration changes shall take effect within 60 seconds of being saved.
generated_requirements.rationale: Directly addresses the configurability quality attribute, ensuring the system can adapt to changing business rules and school-level policies without engineering intervention.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on at least two major cloud platforms (e.g., AWS and Azure) using containerized infrastructure (e.g., Docker/Kubernetes), with no hard-coded platform-specific dependencies in the application layer.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reduces vendor lock-in risk and ensures the system can be migrated or multi-homed across cloud environments as organizational infrastructure strategy evolves.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a synchronous API response to the submitting vendor for every lead submission within 2 seconds of receipt, acknowledging acceptance into the processing pipeline with a unique system-assigned lead tracking ID, even if full scoring and routing have not yet completed asynchronously.
generated_requirements.rationale: Decouples vendor-facing acknowledgment latency from internal processing latency, preventing vendor timeouts while still meeting the internal 5-second end-to-end SLA for routing.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow players to register an account using a unique username and password before accessing the lobby.
generated_requirements.rationale: Player registration is the entry point to the session lifecycle; a unique identity is required to distinguish players in the lobby and during gameplay.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a shared lobby that displays a real-time list of all registered players who are currently available to be challenged.
generated_requirements.rationale: The lobby is the matchmaking hub; players must be able to see available opponents and initiate a game session as described in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a player to select an available opponent from the lobby and send a game invitation, which the opponent must accept or decline before a match begins.
generated_requirements.rationale: Explicit opponent selection and invitation acceptance ensures both players consent to the match, preventing unwanted game starts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present each player with a personal grid on which they must secretly place their fleet of ships before the game begins, enforcing valid placement rules (no overlapping ships, no out-of-bounds placement).
generated_requirements.rationale: Ship placement is a core game mechanic; the rules engine must validate placement to ensure fair and rule-compliant gameplay.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a strict turn-based progression in which only the active player may fire a shot at a coordinate on the opponent's grid, automatically switching turns upon shot resolution.
generated_requirements.rationale: Turn management is fundamental to Battleship; automatic turn switching prevents cheating and ensures game state consistency between both clients.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall evaluate each shot fired and classify it as a hit, miss, or sinking (when the last cell of a ship is hit), updating both players' views of the game state in real time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Hit/miss/sinking detection is the core rules-engine function; both players must receive consistent, synchronised state updates immediately after each shot.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall declare the game over and announce the winner when all ships in one player's fleet have been sunk, or when a player forfeits, and shall return both players to the lobby upon game completion.
generated_requirements.rationale: Victory condition detection and forfeit handling complete the player session lifecycle and ensure a clean transition back to the lobby.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render the game interface using a nautical periscope-style visual frame, display ships as realistic naval vessel images on the grid, and play distinct audio sound effects for hit, miss, and sinking events.
generated_requirements.rationale: The nautical theme, periscope rendering, ship imagery, and audio feedback are explicitly required UI/UX elements that define the product's identity and player experience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support English, Spanish, and French as selectable interface languages, applying the chosen language to all UI text, labels, notifications, and error messages without requiring a restart.
generated_requirements.rationale: Internationalisation across three languages is an explicit requirement to serve the broad international audience described in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall propagate game state updates (shots fired, turn changes, hit/miss/sinking results) to both players' clients within 500 milliseconds under normal network conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Real-time multiplayer synchronisation requires low latency; a 500 ms threshold ensures the game feels responsive and prevents perceptible lag between players.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 10,000 simultaneous active game sessions without degradation of response times beyond the defined performance thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: High concurrency is a key quality attribute; the system must scale to a large international player base without performance degradation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.9% measured on a rolling 30-day basis, permitting no more than approximately 43 minutes of unplanned downtime per month.
generated_requirements.rationale: Continuous availability is an explicit quality attribute; 99.9% uptime ensures the system is accessible to a global audience across all time zones.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically detect a disconnected player and pause the game for up to 60 seconds to allow reconnection, resuming the game from the exact state at disconnection if the player reconnects within that window, or declaring a forfeit otherwise.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fault tolerance for network interruptions is critical in a real-time multiplayer game; graceful reconnection handling prevents unfair game losses due to transient connectivity issues.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ensure that both players always observe an identical, consistent game state; any discrepancy detected between clients shall be resolved by the authoritative server state within one turn cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: Game state consistency is essential for fairness; a server-authoritative model prevents cheating and resolves any client-side divergence.

generated_requirements.text: A new user with no prior knowledge of the system shall be able to complete registration, join the lobby, place ships, and fire their first shot without consulting any external documentation, as validated by first-time user testing with a task-completion rate of at least 90%.
generated_requirements.rationale: Zero-training learnability for a broad international audience is an explicit requirement; the 90% task-completion benchmark provides a measurable usability target.

generated_requirements.text: All user-facing text, date formats, and directional layouts shall be fully externalized into locale resource files, enabling the addition of new languages without changes to application source code.
generated_requirements.rationale: Externalising locale resources decouples language content from code, reducing the cost and risk of adding or updating language support in the future.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on any major cloud platform (AWS, Azure, or GCP) using containerised services, with no dependency on platform-specific proprietary APIs for core game functionality.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cloud portability ensures the system is not locked into a single provider, supporting operational flexibility and disaster-recovery options for a continuously available service.

generated_requirements.text: All player passwords shall be stored using a salted cryptographic hash (minimum bcrypt cost factor 12), and all client-server communication shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Protecting player credentials and in-transit game data is a baseline security requirement, especially given the international user base and account-based registration model.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent a player from firing at a coordinate they have already targeted in the current game, displaying a localised warning message and requiring the player to select a different coordinate.
generated_requirements.rationale: Duplicate-shot prevention is a core rules-engine constraint that maintains game integrity and provides clear feedback consistent with the usability and localisation requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system's lobby player list and in-game state shall be updated via persistent WebSocket connections, falling back to long-polling if WebSocket is unavailable, to maintain real-time synchronisation across all connected clients.
generated_requirements.rationale: A persistent, low-latency communication channel is architecturally necessary to meet the real-time synchronisation and latency performance requirements for a multiplayer game.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow supervisors to create, edit, and delete caller schedules, including specifying shift dates, start times, end times, and assigned callers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow supervisors to view the complete schedules of all callers in a consolidated calendar or list view, filterable by date range and individual caller.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supervisors require visibility across all caller schedules to manage staffing effectively.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow supervisors to advertise open (unfilled) time slots, making them visible to eligible callers within the system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Open slot advertising is an explicitly stated supervisor capability needed to fill staffing gaps.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow callers to view their own individual schedule information, including upcoming shifts, dates, and times, in a read-only format.
generated_requirements.rationale: Callers require self-service access to their scheduling information; access must be read-only to enforce role separation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow callers to view currently advertised open time slots that they are eligible to claim or express interest in.
generated_requirements.rationale: Callers must be able to see open slots published by supervisors to support voluntary shift uptake.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC), ensuring that callers can only access their own scheduling data and cannot create, modify, or delete any schedule records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require all users to authenticate with a unique username and password before accessing any scheduling data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an uptime of at least 99.5% during the TeleServices Center's defined operational hours, with planned maintenance windows scheduled outside of those hours.

generated_requirements.text: All pages and scheduling operations shall load and respond within 3 seconds under normal operational load (up to 100 concurrent users).

generated_requirements.text: A new supervisor user shall be able to learn to create and publish a caller schedule without any formal training within 30 minutes of first use, as validated by usability testing.

generated_requirements.text: A new caller user shall be able to locate and view their own schedule without any formal training within 5 minutes of first use, as validated by usability testing.

generated_requirements.text: The user interface shall follow a consistent visual design language, including clear typography, sufficient color contrast (WCAG 2.1 AA minimum), and logical information hierarchy across all screens.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be fully functional on the current and immediately preceding major versions of Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, Microsoft Edge, and Apple Safari without requiring browser plugins or extensions.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the client browser and the server shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher.

generated_requirements.text: User sessions shall automatically expire after 15 minutes of inactivity, requiring re-authentication to resume access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all supervisor actions that create, modify, or delete schedule records, capturing the user ID, timestamp, and nature of the change, and retain these audit logs for a minimum of 90 days.
