generated_requirements.text: The platform shall ingest messages from the Customer Service Request (CSR) system and route them asynchronously to the Enterprise Asset Management (EAM/Datastream) system without requiring a direct interface between the two systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: This is the core integration contract of the platform — decoupling CSR and EAM via asynchronous message exchange is the primary functional purpose of the system.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall enforce a defined message schema (structure, field types, and mandatory fields) for all messages exchanged between the CSR and EAM/Datastream systems, rejecting any message that does not conform to the schema at ingestion time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Schema validation at ingestion prevents malformed messages from propagating through the pipeline and corrupting downstream systems.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall guarantee ordered (FIFO) delivery of messages within a given logical message sequence or transaction group, ensuring that dependent messages are delivered to EAM/Datastream in the same order they were submitted by the CSR system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Sequencing guarantees are critical for asset management workflows where out-of-order operations (e.g., close before open) would produce invalid state in EAM/Datastream.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall detect failed message deliveries and automatically retry delivery using a configurable exponential back-off policy; messages that exceed the maximum retry threshold shall be moved to a dead-letter queue and flagged for administrator review.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated retry and dead-letter handling are essential failure-recovery workflows that minimize manual intervention and prevent message loss.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall provide System Administrators and Database Administrators with a dashboard that displays real-time message traffic metrics, including messages ingested per minute, messages delivered, messages in queue, and messages in error state.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operational visibility through a real-time dashboard is explicitly required for administrators to monitor message traffic and system health.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall provide an ad-hoc reporting interface that allows System Administrators and Database Administrators to query message transaction history using key performance indicators (KPIs) such as throughput, error rate, average latency, and queue depth over user-defined time ranges.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ad-hoc KPI reporting supports performance analysis and SLA verification, which are stated operational responsibilities of the administrator roles.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall retain all messages — including payload, metadata, routing decisions, delivery status, and timestamps — for a minimum of seven (7) years to support audit and transaction tracking requirements.
generated_requirements.rationale: Long-term message retention is explicitly required for audit purposes; a seven-year window aligns with typical municipal records-retention obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall allow System Administrators to manually resubmit individual messages or batches of messages from the dead-letter queue or error state back into the active processing pipeline after investigation and correction.
generated_requirements.rationale: Manual resubmission is a key error-resolution workflow for administrators, enabling recovery from transient or correctable failures without data loss.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall process and deliver at least 95% of all ingested messages to their target system within 5 seconds of receipt under normal operating load, and within 30 seconds under peak load conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Strict latency thresholds are required to meet the City of Chicago's operational SLAs for real-time service request processing between CSR and EAM.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall sustain a minimum throughput of 500 messages per second during peak operational periods without degradation of message ordering guarantees or delivery latency SLAs.
generated_requirements.rationale: A defined peak throughput target ensures the platform is sized to handle high transaction volumes characteristic of a large municipal enterprise environment.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall maintain a minimum availability of 99.9% (no more than approximately 8.7 hours of unplanned downtime per year) during regular business operations, as measured on a rolling 12-month basis.
generated_requirements.rationale: Near-continuous availability is explicitly stated as a design goal; 99.9% is the standard threshold that operationalizes 'near-continuous' for SLA enforcement.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall support horizontal scaling by allowing additional processing nodes or broker instances to be added to the cluster transparently — without service interruption, message loss, or reordering — to accommodate planned capacity growth and unplanned workload spikes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transparent, non-disruptive resource addition is explicitly required to support dynamic scaling without impacting running workloads or message integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall recover from a single node or broker failure within 60 seconds, automatically redistributing in-flight messages to healthy nodes with no message loss and no manual administrator intervention required.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated failover with a defined recovery time objective (RTO) is necessary to uphold the availability SLA and ensure continuity of CSR-to-EAM message flow.

generated_requirements.text: All administrative users (System Administrators and Database Administrators) shall be authenticated via multi-factor authentication (MFA) before accessing the platform's management console or reporting interfaces.
generated_requirements.rationale: MFA for administrative access enforces the authentication control at the UI/administrative layer, protecting privileged operations from unauthorized access.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) such that System Administrators may configure routing rules and manage queues, Database Administrators may access reporting and audit logs, and no role may perform actions outside its defined permission set.
generated_requirements.rationale: RBAC operationalizes the authorization control, ensuring least-privilege access across the two distinct administrator roles described in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: All message data in transit between the CSR system, the platform, and the EAM/Datastream system shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher; all message data at rest (including queued messages and the audit log) shall be encrypted using AES-256 or equivalent.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption at both transport and storage layers is explicitly required; specifying TLS 1.2+ and AES-256 provides unambiguous, enforceable cryptographic standards.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall validate the integrity of every message using a cryptographic hash or digital signature upon ingestion and again upon delivery, rejecting and quarantining any message whose integrity check fails.
generated_requirements.rationale: Message-level integrity checking is one of the four explicitly stated security controls; it must be applied at both the message ingestion and delivery layers.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall generate an immutable audit log entry for every message lifecycle event — including receipt, routing decision, delivery attempt, delivery success or failure, retry, dead-letter queuing, and manual resubmission — capturing the event timestamp, actor identity, and message identifier.
generated_requirements.rationale: A comprehensive, immutable audit trail satisfies the auditability requirement and supports forensic investigation, compliance reporting, and transaction tracking.

generated_requirements.text: The platform's configuration, routing rules, and integration adapters shall be modifiable without requiring a full system restart or causing interruption to in-flight message processing, supporting zero-downtime configuration changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Zero-downtime configuration changes are essential for an enterprise platform with a 99.9% availability SLA, enabling operational updates without violating uptime commitments.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall expose a documented API and configuration schema that allows integration adapters for new source or target systems to be developed, tested, and deployed independently of the core messaging engine, without modifying the engine's codebase.
generated_requirements.rationale: A pluggable adapter model reduces the cost and risk of future integrations, supporting the platform's long-term extensibility as the City of Chicago's system landscape evolves.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict access to authorized IBM Corporate Community Relations (CCR) employees only, authenticated via IBM intranet credentials.
generated_requirements.rationale: Access control is a primary quality attribute; only authorized CCR personnel should be able to view sensitive community grants data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control, distinguishing between standard CCR employees and CCR management, granting each role access only to the reports and data views appropriate to their authorization level.
generated_requirements.rationale: CCR employees and management have distinct reporting needs and data sensitivity constraints that require differentiated permissions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically refresh its underlying community grants data on a weekly basis, ensuring that all reports reflect data no older than seven calendar days.
generated_requirements.rationale: Weekly data refresh is explicitly required to keep information current for decision-making by CCR users and management.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a clearly visible timestamp indicating the date and time of the most recent data refresh on every report view.
generated_requirements.rationale: Users must be able to assess data freshness at a glance to trust the accuracy of the reports they are analyzing.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to generate summarized community grants reports by selecting report parameters (e.g., date range, region, grant type) through a point-and-click interface, without requiring any manual query or scripting.
generated_requirements.rationale: The point-and-click report generation interface is a core functional requirement enabling non-technical CCR users to self-serve reporting needs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enable users to export any generated report in Microsoft Excel format (.xlsx) with full data fidelity, preserving all column headers, data types, and numeric precision present in the on-screen report.
generated_requirements.rationale: Microsoft Excel is a primary export format explicitly called out in the use case; data fidelity is critical for downstream analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enable users to export any generated report in Lotus 1-2-3 format (.wks/.wk1) with full data fidelity, preserving all column headers, data types, and numeric precision present in the on-screen report.
generated_requirements.rationale: Lotus 1-2-3 is explicitly listed as a required export format in the use case description, ensuring compatibility with legacy IBM tooling.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete any report export operation and deliver the downloadable file to the user within 10 seconds for reports containing up to 10,000 rows of data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Export is a primary user-facing feature; slow export times would degrade usability and undermine the 'minimal effort' requirement stated in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render any requested summarized report on-screen within 5 seconds under normal intranet load conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsive report generation is essential for a productive user experience, particularly for management users who may need rapid access to data.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to IBM corporate branding guidelines and CCR-specific visual standards, including approved color palettes, typography, logo placement, and layout conventions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adherence to IBM and CCR branding is an explicit acceptance criterion stated in the use case and ensures organizational consistency.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be sufficiently intuitive that a new authorized user can independently navigate the full report generation and export workflow after a single unassisted use, without requiring formal training.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly states users should be able to navigate the system independently after a single use, making learnability a measurable acceptance criterion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be accessible exclusively through the IBM intranet and shall not be reachable from the public internet under any circumstances.
generated_requirements.rationale: Restricting access to the IBM intranet is a fundamental security boundary that protects sensitive community grants data from external exposure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all user authentication events, report generation actions, and data export operations, retaining audit logs for a minimum of 90 days.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit logging supports accountability and forensic investigation in the event of unauthorized access or data misuse involving sensitive grants data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an availability of at least 99% during IBM standard business hours (Monday–Friday, 07:00–19:00 local time), excluding pre-approved maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: As an internal reporting tool relied upon by CCR employees and management, unplanned downtime during business hours directly impacts operational decision-making.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be implemented using a modular, layered architecture that allows the data ingestion/refresh component, the report generation engine, and the export module to be updated or replaced independently without requiring a full system redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: A modular architecture reduces the cost and risk of future changes, such as adding new export formats or updating the data refresh schedule.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate the integrity and completeness of each weekly data refresh, and shall alert the system administrator via email notification if the refresh fails or if the ingested dataset is missing more than 1% of expected records.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data accuracy is a key quality attribute; automated validation and alerting ensures administrators can remediate data issues before users encounter stale or incomplete reports.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support concurrent usage by at least 50 authenticated users simultaneously without degradation of report generation or export response times beyond the defined performance thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: As an organization-wide internal tool, the system must handle concurrent access from multiple CCR employees and managers without performance bottlenecks.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on IBM's standard intranet web hosting infrastructure and shall not require the installation of any client-side plugins, extensions, or software beyond a standard IBM-approved web browser.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support two distinct user roles — League Commissioner and Team Owner — each with a separate, role-specific set of permissions and UI controls.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly defines two primary roles with different privileges; role separation is foundational to all downstream permission and workflow requirements.

generated_requirements.text: Team Owners shall be able to add, drop, and trade NFL players on their roster, with each transaction recorded with a timestamp, the acting user, and the resulting roster state.
generated_requirements.rationale: Roster management (add/drop/trade) is the core workflow for team owners; audit-trail recording supports data integrity and dispute resolution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display up-to-date NFL player statistics and calculated fantasy points on player profile and roster pages, refreshing data at intervals no greater than 60 seconds during live game windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: Real-time statistics are a primary decision-making input for team owners; the refresh cadence ensures data remains actionable during live games.

generated_requirements.text: The League Commissioner shall be able to configure and modify league-wide settings including, but not limited to, scoring system rules, roster size limits, trade review periods, waiver priority order, and playoff bracket format.
generated_requirements.rationale: Commissioner governance over league settings is explicitly required; enumerating configurable parameters ensures complete coverage of the governance model.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a configurable trade review period (set by the Commissioner) during which any league member may file a veto vote; trades shall not be applied to rosters until the review period expires without a successful veto.
generated_requirements.rationale: Trade dispute resolution is a key governance workflow; a veto mechanism prevents unfair roster manipulation and supports league integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a league message board where authenticated members can create posts, reply to threads, and where the Commissioner can pin, hide, or delete any post for moderation purposes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Community engagement via a message board is explicitly called out; moderation controls are necessary to maintain a safe and relevant discussion environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send in-app and email notifications to affected Team Owners when a trade offer is received, a waiver claim is processed, a roster transaction is completed, or a new message board post is made in a thread they are subscribed to.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely notifications keep participants engaged and informed of actions that directly affect their team or community interactions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use optimistic locking or an equivalent concurrency control mechanism to prevent conflicting simultaneous roster transactions (e.g., two owners claiming the same waiver player at the same instant), ensuring only one transaction succeeds and the other receives an immediate, descriptive error.
generated_requirements.rationale: Concurrent waiver claims and trades during peak NFL game periods are a critical data-integrity risk; concurrency control is mandatory to maintain consistent roster state.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide intuitive navigation allowing authenticated users to move between the league home page, individual team pages, player search, standings, and message board in no more than two clicks from any primary page.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case highlights intuitive navigation as a design goal; the two-click rule is a measurable usability standard that reduces cognitive load.

generated_requirements.text: All interactive page loads and roster transaction responses shall complete within 2 seconds at the 95th percentile under a concurrent load of up to 500 simultaneous users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid response times are explicitly required; the 2-second/P95 threshold is an industry-standard benchmark for web application responsiveness under realistic peak load.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall maintain a minimum uptime of 99.9% (measured monthly), with scheduled maintenance windows restricted to Tuesday mornings between 03:00–05:00 ET to avoid NFL game-day traffic.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability throughout the NFL season is explicitly required; restricting maintenance to low-traffic windows protects the user experience during peak periods.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users via username/password with mandatory multi-factor authentication (MFA) for Commissioner accounts, and shall enforce HTTPS for all client-server communication.
generated_requirements.rationale: Commissioner accounts carry elevated privileges that could disrupt the entire league if compromised; MFA and HTTPS are baseline security controls to protect sensitive operations and data in transit.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) such that Team Owners cannot access or invoke any Commissioner-only configuration endpoints, and all authorization decisions are validated server-side.
generated_requirements.rationale: Client-side-only permission enforcement is insufficient; server-side RBAC prevents privilege escalation attacks that could allow a team owner to alter league settings.

generated_requirements.text: All roster transaction records, scoring results, and league configuration changes shall be persisted in a transactional database with ACID guarantees, and the system shall perform automated backups at least every 24 hours with a recovery point objective (RPO) of no more than 1 hour.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data integrity for transactions and scoring is critical; ACID compliance prevents partial writes, and a 1-hour RPO limits data loss in the event of a system failure.

generated_requirements.text: The system architecture shall support horizontal scaling of application and data-caching tiers to handle traffic spikes of up to 5× baseline load during Sunday NFL game windows without degradation in response time SLAs.
generated_requirements.rationale: NFL game days produce predictable, extreme traffic spikes; horizontal scalability ensures performance SLAs are maintained without over-provisioning infrastructure year-round.

generated_requirements.text: The web application shall be fully functional on the two most recent major versions of Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge browsers, and shall be responsive and usable on screen widths from 375px (mobile) to 2560px (desktop).
generated_requirements.rationale: Users access fantasy platforms across diverse devices and browsers; cross-browser and responsive design support maximizes accessibility and reduces support burden.

generated_requirements.text: The codebase shall maintain a minimum of 80% unit and integration test coverage, and all Commissioner-facing configuration modules shall have dedicated regression test suites to facilitate safe future modifications.
generated_requirements.rationale: A fantasy platform evolves with each NFL season's rule changes; high test coverage reduces regression risk and lowers the cost of ongoing maintenance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all security-relevant events — including login attempts, failed authentications, role-permission checks, and Commissioner configuration changes — to a tamper-evident, centralized audit log retained for a minimum of 12 months.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit logs are essential for detecting unauthorized access, investigating disputes, and demonstrating compliance; 12-month retention covers a full NFL season plus off-season review.

generated_requirements.text: The message board shall include an automated content moderation filter that flags or hides posts containing prohibited language, with flagged posts queued for Commissioner review within 5 minutes of submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: Community moderation is necessary to maintain a respectful environment; automated filtering reduces the manual burden on the Commissioner and ensures rapid response to policy violations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present a real-time league standings page that recalculates and displays updated win/loss records, points-for, and points-against totals within 90 seconds of any scoring event being ingested from the NFL data feed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Standings accuracy during live games is a high-value feature for user engagement; the 90-second SLA balances data freshness with processing overhead from the scoring pipeline.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized finance staff and managers at branch, complex, district, and regional levels to create, edit, and submit annual budget submissions through a structured multi-step workflow.
generated_requirements.rationale: Core functional capability of the RFS — budget submission is the primary use case across all four organizational levels.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall load revenue and expense data directly from the Business Data Warehouse (BDW) at the start of each budgeting cycle, ensuring that all budget submissions are pre-populated with the most current BDW data.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly states that revenue and expense data is loaded from the BDW; this integration is foundational to the data entry workflow.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support annual updates to budget calculation formulae prior to the start of each budgeting cycle, and shall version-control each set of formulae so that historical submissions can be recalculated or audited against the formulae in effect at the time of submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: Formulae are updated each year; versioning ensures auditability and prevents accidental application of incorrect formulae to prior-period data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the central User Administration System (UAS) to authenticate all users and retrieve their assigned roles and organizational hierarchy positions before granting access to any system functionality.
generated_requirements.rationale: Access control is driven by UAS integration; authentication and role retrieval must occur before any data or workflow is exposed to the user.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) such that each user can only view, enter, modify, or approve budget data for the organizational unit(s) and hierarchy level(s) assigned to them in the UAS, with no cross-level or cross-unit data visibility permitted unless explicitly authorized.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case requires strict permission boundaries; users must be confined to their own scope within the PCG hierarchy to protect sensitive financial data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement a hierarchical budget approval chain whereby completed budget submissions at the branch level are routed upward through complex, district, and regional levels for review and approval, with each level able to approve, reject, or return submissions with comments.
generated_requirements.rationale: A multi-level organizational hierarchy implies a corresponding approval workflow; capturing approval, rejection, and feedback actions is essential for process completeness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an administrative interface that allows designated administrators to update, activate, and deactivate annual budget calculation formulae, with changes taking effect only after explicit confirmation and only for the designated upcoming budgeting cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: Annual formula management requires a controlled admin workflow to prevent accidental mid-cycle changes that would corrupt in-progress submissions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a complete audit log of all user actions, including data entry, formula updates, submission events, and approval decisions, recording the user identity, timestamp, organizational unit, and before/after values for any data modification.
generated_requirements.rationale: Financial systems require full auditability for compliance and dispute resolution; the audit log supports both security monitoring and data integrity verification.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the user's browser and the RFS server shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher, and all sensitive budget data at rest shall be encrypted using AES-256 or an equivalent approved algorithm.
generated_requirements.rationale: Financial data is highly sensitive; encryption in transit and at rest is a baseline security requirement aligned with ENET Securities' intranet standards.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce session timeout after no more than 15 minutes of user inactivity, requiring re-authentication via the UAS before the session is resumed, to prevent unauthorized access to unattended sessions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Inactivity-based session expiry is a standard security control for intranet financial applications to mitigate insider threat and accidental exposure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an availability of at least 99.5% uptime during the defined annual budgeting period (as configured by administrators), and at least 99.0% uptime outside of the budgeting period, excluding pre-approved maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly calls out high availability during the critical budgeting period; quantified SLAs are needed to make this requirement testable and enforceable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall respond to any user-initiated page load or data retrieval request within 3 seconds under normal load conditions, and within 6 seconds under peak budgeting-period load, as measured at the server side with up to 200 concurrent users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsiveness during peak budgeting periods is explicitly required; concrete thresholds and concurrency levels make this requirement measurable.

generated_requirements.text: BDW data loads into the RFS shall complete within 30 minutes of initiation for a full organizational dataset, and incremental/delta loads shall complete within 5 minutes, to ensure data freshness at the start of each budgeting session.
generated_requirements.rationale: Slow data loads would block finance staff from beginning their submissions; time-bound SLAs on BDW integration protect the budgeting workflow.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all revenue and expense data entered by users against the active budgeting formulae and BDW reference data in real time, displaying clear, field-level error messages for any values that fall outside permitted ranges or violate formula constraints, before allowing submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: Real-time validation prevents corrupt or inconsistent data from propagating through the approval chain, directly supporting the data integrity quality attribute.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to ENET Securities' intranet UI standards, including approved color palettes, typography, layout grids, and component libraries, and shall be fully functional on all browser versions designated as supported in the current ENET Securities intranet browser compatibility policy.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly mandates conformance to ENET Securities' intranet standards for look, feel, and browser compatibility; this is a non-negotiable deployment constraint.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed using a modular, layered architecture with documented APIs for BDW integration and UAS integration, such that either external system can be upgraded or replaced without requiring changes to the core RFS application logic.
generated_requirements.rationale: Annual formula updates and potential changes to BDW or UAS make maintainability critical; loose coupling via documented APIs reduces the cost and risk of future changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide finance staff and managers with a consolidated, read-only summary dashboard that aggregates budget submission status and key revenue/expense totals across all organizational units within the user's authorized scope, updated in near real time (within 5 minutes of any submission event).
generated_requirements.rationale: Managers overseeing multiple units need visibility into submission progress and financial totals; a dashboard supports informed decision-making during the budgeting cycle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall preserve the referential integrity of all budget submission data by preventing deletion or modification of submitted records once they have entered the approval chain, allowing only authorized administrators to perform corrections via a controlled amendment process with full audit trail.
generated_requirements.rationale: Once submitted, budget data must remain stable and trustworthy throughout the approval chain; immutability with a controlled amendment process protects data integrity and supports auditability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support scheduled and on-demand export of budget submission data and audit logs in at least CSV and PDF formats, accessible to users with appropriate administrative or reporting roles.
generated_requirements.rationale: Finance teams routinely need to extract data for offline analysis, reporting, and compliance purposes; export functionality is a standard requirement for budgeting systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable exclusively within PCG's intranet environment and shall not expose any application endpoints, data, or APIs to the public internet, in compliance with ENET Securities' intranet deployment standards.
generated_requirements.rationale: The RFS is explicitly described as an intranet-based application; restricting all exposure to the intranet perimeter is a fundamental security and compliance constraint.
